urn:contentItem:6JK2-K5D3-S2HV-T4VF-00000-00Primoris Services Corporation (NYSE:PRIM) Securities Investigation Initiated Over Q1 Results and EBITDA Guidance Revision2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:49ZLexisNexisPrimoris Services Corporation (NYSE:PRIM) Securities Investigation Initiated Over Q1 Results and EBITDA Guidance Revision

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

14 May 2026 02:42 EDT

NEW YORK CITY, NY / ACCESS Newswire / May 12, 2026 — Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC announced it is reviewing potential claims on behalf of investors who purchased Primoris Services Corporation (NYSE:PRIM) securities. Primoris reported on May 5, 2026 that first-quarter results were below estimates and that full-year adjusted EBITDA guidance was lowered from $560-$580 million to $480-$500 million due to weaker renewable-energy activity, delayed starts, and higher renewable costs. The news contributed to a 50.11% decline in the stock to $101.23 on May 6, 2026, as investors digested the impact of the revision. Investors with information can visit bgandg.com/PRIM or contact Peretz Bronstein or Nathan Miller at 917-590-0911. The firm emphasizes contingency-fee representation and notes it has recovered hundreds of millions for investors nationwide; Peretz Bronstein commented, "Our practice centers on restoring investor capital and ensuring corporate accountability, which serves to uphold the essential integrity of the marketplace."

IN TODAY'S REPORT:

SECTION 1 PRIMORIS SERVICES RECENT STORIES ON DIVIDENDS:

SECTION 1 Primoris Services Recent Stories on Dividends:

May 08: Primoris Services quarterly dividends steady

Primoris Services today announced a quarterly dividend of 8.0c per share for Q1/2026; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q4/2025) dividend of 8.0c. The ex-dividend date is Tuesday, June 30, 2026 and the record date is Tuesday, June 30, 2026 and it is payable on Wednesday, July 15.

May 08: Primoris Services quarterly dividends steady

Primoris Services today announced a quarterly dividend of 8.0c per share for Q1/2026; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q4/2025) dividend of 8.0c. The ex-dividend date is Tuesday, June 30, 2026 and the record date is Tuesday, June 30, 2026 and it is payable on Wednesday, July 15.

May 08: Primoris Services quarterly dividends steady

Primoris Services today announced a quarterly dividend of 8.0c per share for Q1/2026; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q4/2025) dividend of 8.0c. The ex-dividend date is Tuesday, June 30, 2026 and the record date is Tuesday, June 30, 2026 and it is payable on Wednesday, July 15.

urn:contentItem:6JK2-K5D3-S2HV-T4VJ-00000-00Primoris Services Corporation (NYSE:PRIM) Investigated by Law Firm Over Q1 Miss and Lowered EBITDA Forecast2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:49ZLexisNexisPrimoris Services Corporation (NYSE:PRIM) Investigated by Law Firm Over Q1 Miss and Lowered EBITDA Forecast

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

14 May 2026 02:42 EDT

NEW YORK CITY, NY / ACCESS Newswire / May 12, 2026 — Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC has launched a review on behalf of Primoris Services Corporation (NYSE:PRIM) investors. Investors are invited to visit bgandg.com/PRIM for information about the inquiry. Primoris reported May 5, 2026 that first-quarter results fell short of expectations and that full-year adjusted EBITDA guidance was cut from $560-$580 million to $480-$500 million, attributing the reduction to lower renewable-energy activity, delayed project starts, and higher costs on renewable projects. After the disclosure, the stock declined to close at $101.23 on May 6, 2026, down $101.69 per share, or 50.11%. Investors with information are urged to contact Peretz Bronstein or Nathan Miller at 917-590-0911 or visit bgandg.com/PRIM. Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman notes it handles securities actions on a contingency basis and has recovered hundreds of millions for investors nationwide. "Our practice centers on restoring investor capital and ensuring corporate accountability, which serves to uphold the essential integrity of the marketplace."

value.Title: He's building a business in Buffalo after a decade away

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK5-3F93-RVK1-3021-00000-00He's building a business in Buffalo after a decade away2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:49ZLexisNexisHe's building a business in Buffalo after a decade awayLian Bunny

Traveling abroad sparked Jared Perram's passion for renewable energy, but taking charge of his own career and happiness led him to plant roots back home in Buffalo.

Over 16 years ago, Perram, an Orchard Park native, studied for a semester through the University of Virginia's Semester at Sea program. A converted cruise liner became the students' floating classroom for three months as they visited about nine different countries.

It was his first exposure to the renewable energy investments in many Scandinavian countries. He wanted to pair that with his business degree from Buffalo State University.

"It just felt very obvious to me and it felt like a path or an industry that was emerging that I could certainly get in on the bottom floor," he said. "I see logic in the renewables and I see logic in how I can pair all of this up."

Why it matters: Buffalo benefits from locals moving away and returning home, as they bring new perspectives, experiences and an appreciation for WesternNew York with them. Entrepreneurs like Perram are adding to our small business ecosystem and economy.

After moving away from the region for a decade to work in renewable energy in Charlotte and California, he moved to Rochester to be closer to home and due to New York's need for professionals like him in solar programs.

He left that role after a few years, stepped away from renewables entirely, moved to Buffalo and started planning his next move. In 2024, he began a consulting business that didn't take off and had trouble finding work he was excited about.

"One morning I woke up... and just decided, you know what, I'm done putting any of my future, my happiness or my wellbeing in anyone else's hands," he said. "I'm just going all in on a cleaning business."

He began Transparent Property Services, a cleaning and related services business, in July 2025. He's still a one-man operation but has grown the mobile business from around three jobs a week to two jobs per day booked out through mid-June.

As his full-time job, he focuses on customer service, from good communication to showing up on time to fixing mistakes if they happen. He uses the combination of his experiences - working with contractors through renewable energy and project management roles and landscaping and construction jobs when he was younger.

Perram likes that his work and company are in his own hands and are in an "AI-proof" industry, while utilizing AI integrations for back-end operations to boost efficiency.

He's also focused on search-engine optimization, leading him to make moves that aren't requirements for his business like getting Better Business Bureau accredited and obtaining his contractor's license through the City of Buffalo.

His main clients are individual homeowners, and he's found that people here care and want to invest in how their home looks, regardless of whether they're middle-class or luxury-residence owners. Folks also value hard work.

"I think that this type of market, Buffalo, is an interesting market for businesses like this, for sort of blue collar, independent businesses," Perram said. "It is a community that embraces hard work. I don't know that I could start this company in California."

Since returning to Buffalo, he's also had a deeper appreciation for many of the region's benefits, from proximity to family to the bigger city feel within a smaller city. The cost of living is preferable to California's, he said, and he can be in the forest or country with his canoe via a 15-minute drive.

"I think I appreciate much more now this kind of little corner of the world that we have," he said. "I love what the city's doing and I want to be a part of it."

Do you know a regional business professional who exemplifies what Buffalo could use more of, from a local returning home to a business owner with no local ties building a company here? Email Lbunny@bizjournals.com with your suggestion.

Did you find this article useful? Why not subscribe to Buffalo Business First for more articles?

value.Byline: We all want to leave the environment in a better place than when we got here but at what cost?
value.Title: Guest essay: Albany's solar push is putting our farmland at risk
value.Overview: Livingston County News: Web Edition Articles (Geneseo, New York), VOICES, 751words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGB-FKH3-SD6M-J49K-00000-00Guest essay: Albany's solar push is putting our farmland at risk2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:49ZLexisNexisVOICESGuest essay: Albany's solar push is putting our farmland at riskWe all want to leave the environment in a better place than when we got here but at what cost?

That question is becoming increasingly urgent as New York state expands its approach to large-scale solar development. While the goal may be well-intentioned, the process we've built is straying further from the democratic principles, transparency and local accountability that should guide it.

At the center of this issue is the Office of Renewable Energy Siting (ORES), a state body that now holds sweeping, unilateral authority over the approval of large-scale solar projects.

To understand how we arrived here, we need to look back. In 2020, New York overhauled its siting process in response to delays under the former Article 10 framework.

That system, though imperfect, provided multiple layers of review and local input. In its place, the state enacted Article 94-c and created ORES, consolidating decision-making power into a single office with the ability to override local zoning laws it deems "unreasonably burdensome."

The intention was to accelerate development. The reality has been the erosion of local voice.

And just as communities began grappling with this shift, the state doubled down. The Renewable Action through Project Interconnection and Deployment (RAPID) Act further expanded ORES authority, adding insult to injury for many municipalities already feeling sidelined. Rather than correcting course or improving transparency, the RAPID Act reinforced a top-down approach that leaves little room for meaningful local involvement.

We cannot ignore what is happening across our rural communities. Investment groups are buying up productive agricultural land, not to sustain it, but to convert it into industrial-scale solar fields.

These are not small, community-driven projects; they are large, profit-driven developments. And they are being fueled in part by government subsidies, allowing outside companies to benefit financially while fundamentally reshaping the character and long-term viability of our local economies.

At the same time, there is a glaring inconsistency in environmental oversight. If residents sought to alter their own property in a way that might disturb endangered habitats, they would face intense scrutiny from the Department of Environmental Conservation. Yet when large-scale solar developments move forward under the banner of "green energy," that same level of visible scrutiny often appears lacking. The limited and unclear role of DEC in these approvals raises serious concerns about whether environmental standards are being applied consistently.

We must be honest with ourselves: Not everything done in the name of "green energy" is environmentally friendly in practice. The clearing of open land, the disruption of habitats and the loss of farmland all have real consequences. Seeing what is being done under the guise of progress, without sufficient transparency or accountability, is a tragedy for many of the communities living through it.

Equally troubling is the lack of transparency surrounding ORES decision-making. Determinations about what constitutes an "unreasonably burdensome" local law are often made without clear standards or adequate explanation. Public comment periods exist, but too often they feel like procedural formalities rather than genuine opportunities for input. Residents and local officials are left with decisions that affect their communities for decades, yet little understanding of how those decisions were reached.

This is not an argument against renewable energy. It is an argument for doing it right. A process that sidelines communities, lacks transparency and raises legitimate environmental concerns is not sustainablepolitically, socially or environmentally.

We need a better balance. That means restoring a meaningful role for local governments, ensuring that community concerns are not dismissed but seriously considered. It means demanding greater transparency from ORES, with clear, consistent standards and open communication. And it means ensuring that environmental oversight is rigorous and applied equally no matter the type of development.

New York has an opportunity to lead not just in energy production, but in how we manage that transition. If we fail to bring communities into the process, we risk losing more than farmland we risk losing public trust. And without that trust, even the best intentions will fall short.

Assemblywoman Andrea Bailey, R-Geneseo, represents the 133rd Assembly District, which includes Livingston County and parts of Monroe, Ontario, Steuben and Wyoming counties.

value.Title: Data on Wind Energy Described by Researchers at University of Rey Juan Carlos (Aerodynamic Enhancement of Airfoils for Wind Energy Applications Using Active Fluid Gurney Flaps)
value.Overview: Energy Daily News, ENERGY - WIND ENERGY, 607words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHV-RSJ3-S0JT-N14H-00000-00Data on Wind Energy Described by Researchers at University of Rey Juan Carlos (Aerodynamic Enhancement of Airfoils for Wind Energy Applications Using Active Fluid Gurney Flaps)2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:49ZLexisNexisENERGY - WIND ENERGYData on Wind Energy Described by Researchers at University of Rey Juan Carlos (Aerodynamic Enhancement of Airfoils for Wind Energy Applications Using Active Fluid Gurney Flaps)


2026 MAY 08 (NewsRx) -- By a News Reporter-Staff News Editor at Energy Daily News -- Data detailed on Energy - Wind Energy have been presented. According to news reporting originating from Madrid, Spain, by NewsRx correspondents, research stated, "Rising global energy demand underscores the need for more efficient renewable power generation, yet further expansion of wind farms is often constrained by geographical and environmental limitations. Enhancing the aerodynamic efficiency of individual turbine blades, therefore, represents a promising strategy to improve overall energy capture."


Financial supporters for this research include Rey Juan Carlos University (URJC), European Union (EU), Comunidad de Madrid, Consejerimath;a de Educacion, Ciencia y Universidades.
Our news editors obtained a quote from the research from the University of Rey Juan Carlos, "This work investigates the aerodynamic performance of an active fluid Gurney flap (AFGF), a flow-control concept inspired by conventional Gurney flaps (GF) but employing trailing-edge jet injection to actively manipulate the pressure field. Unlike passive devices, the active fluid Gurney flap allows real-time control of aerodynamic loading through modulation of the injection pressure, enabling adaptive performance under different operating conditions. A two-dimensional computational fluid dynamics (CFD) framework was developed in ansys fluent to analyze the Active Fluid Gurney Flap on an S809 airfoil at a Reynolds number of Re = 1 & times; 10 6. Unsteady Reynolds-averaged Navier-Stokes (URANS) simulations were conducted for three configurations: a clean airfoil, a conventional Gurney flap, and the proposed active fluid Gurney flap. The results show that the active fluid Gurney flap substantially modifies the pressure distribution by enhancing suction on the suction side and increasing diffusion on the pressure side. This redistribution leads to higher circulation and, consequently, a significant lift augmentation while maintaining controllable aerodynamic behavior. The findings demonstrate that the active fluid Gurney flap provides a flexible and efficient mechanism for aerodynamic performance enhancement, outperforming traditional passive high-lift devices."
According to the news editors, the research concluded: "Due to its controllability and geometric reversibility, the active fluid Gurney flap represents a promising active flow-control strategy with potential applications in wind turbine blade design to improve aerodynamic efficiency and, by extension, their power output potential."
This research has been peer-reviewed.
For more information on this research see: Aerodynamic Enhancement of Airfoils for Wind Energy Applications Using Active Fluid Gurney Flaps. Journal of Fluids Engineering, 2026;148(5). Journal of Fluids Engineering can be contacted at: Asme, Two Park Ave, New York, NY 10016-5990, USA.
The news editors report that additional information may be obtained by contacting Mario Lucas Jerez, University of Rey Juan Carlos, Teoria Senal & Comunicac & Sistemas Telemat & Comp, Camino Molino 5, Fuenlabrada 28942, Madrid, Spain.
The direct object identifier (DOI) for that additional information is: https://doi.org/10.1115/1.4071005. This DOI is a link to an online electronic document that is either free or for purchase, and can be your direct source for a journal article and its citation.
Keywords for this news article include: Madrid, Spain, Europe, Energy, Wind Energy, University of Rey Juan Carlos.
Our reports deliver fact-based news of research and discoveries from around the world. Copyright 2026, NewsRx LLC

value.Title: ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. - SECTOR INSIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHM-8873-SHNH-218W-00000-00ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. - SECTOR INSIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:49ZLexisNexisSTOCKECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. - SECTOR INSIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD.

PermID4296560718Websitehttp://www.ecotricity.co.ukIndustryEnergy & Renewables

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. [3 Press Releases]

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD.: GOOD NEWS AND BAD NEWS: OUR BREAKING THE LINK CAMPAIGN IS WORKING - BUT WE NEED TO GO FURTHER

Britain's energy market is broken. For years, we've been campaigning to "break the link" between the soaring cost of fossil gas and the price of the cheap, green electricity we generate right here in Britain.

Now, the message is finally hitting home in the corridors of Westminster.

There was a flurry of excitement in mid-April 2026 as the chancellor, Rachel Reeves, announced that the government was looking at ways to break the link between electricity and fossil gas prices.

Source: Company Website

+ ECOTALK REINTRODUCE BEAVERS TO EAST SUSSEX AFTER A 400-YEAR ABSENCE

We've got amazing news from Ecotalk this spring with the release of a family of (extremely cute!) beavers into Bowyers Wood, East Sussex.

Chris Packham, Ecotalk's Chief Ecologist, was there to help the beavers get settled in. He said: "I just can't remember when I was last so excited about meeting a rodent! Big, fantastic rodents! What a fantastic day for Bowyers. And massive thanks to all our Ecotalk customers for making this possible."

Source: Company Website

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. OUR DAY WITH THE YOUNG GREEN BRITON CHALLENGE

Four members of the Ecotricity team recently took the hot seats as the judges at Stroud High School for entries to the Young Green Briton Challenge: a national programme that transforms students' climate ideas into real-world action.

For the 'Eco Dragon's Den', our team drew on their real-world expertise in green energy and sustainability. They used this to guide and challenge students on the impact and feasibility of their projects, offering constructive feedback to help sharpen each idea.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ OCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP [3 Press Releases]

+ OCTOPUS ENERGY GENERATION TO INVEST $500 MILLION TO REMOVE POLLUTING CO₂ FROM THE ATMOSPHERE

Major US deal to help cut up to 50 million tonnes of CO₂ - equivalent to eliminating the annual greenhouse gas emissions of New York City

Carbon credits to be snapped up by Google, Meta and McKinsey

Pushes Octopus closer to its ambitious $2bn US investment target, with a significant proportion in California's clean tech scene

London / San Francisco, 30 April 2026 - Octopus Energy Generation, one of Europe's leading renewables investors, is ramping up efforts to slash CO₂ pollution at scale - inking a major US deal that will help remove up to 50 million tonnes of carbon dioxide from the air.

Octopus's fund management team is set to invest $500 million in afforestation and reforestation projects in the US developed by public benefit and climate technology company Living Carbon.

Source: Company Website

+ POWERED UP: OCTOPUS AND MUIRHOUSE SLASH ENERGY BILLS FOR SCOTTISH SOCIAL HOMES BY £200

Muirhouse becomes one of Scotland's first housing associations to roll out Octopus Energy's groundbreaking Tenant Power tariff

UK's first green tech energy tariff that rewards both social housing landlords and tenants

Slashes electricity bills for social housing tenants by around £200 a year

Helping green home upgrades deliver real returns for landlords

London/Edinburgh, 29th April 2026 - Octopus Energy, the UK's largest energy supplier, has partnered with Muirhouse Housing Association to bring its groundbreaking Tenant Power tariff to over 30 social homes in Scotland.

Source: Company Website

+ 1 MILLION CUSTOMERS FOR EVERY TENTACLE: OCTOPUS ENERGY SUPERCHARGES TO 8 MILLION CUSTOMERS IN THE UK

The UK's largest energy supplier announces hitting 8 million home energy customers, with 2500 new customers joining daily

Market leading support for customers now includes You Pay We Pay, OctoAssist fund and the extension of their electric blanket scheme for Winter 2026

The milestone coincides with a doubling in heat pump sales and a 0.75 rise in solar sales as households shift to trusting in home grown power

London, 24th April 2026 - Octopus Energy has surpassed 8 million customers in the UK, further cementing its position as the nation's largest energy supplier.

With 2500 customers switching to Octopus every day, the gap has widened since Octopus claimed the top spot in January 2025, the first time the market lead had changed hands since privatisation in 1986.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: Laki: The man from Rwampara who guards the ocean floor

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHJ-N7R3-S869-K0YV-00000-00Laki: The man from Rwampara who guards the ocean floor2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:49ZLexisNexisLaki: The man from Rwampara who guards the ocean floor

You have probably never heard of the International Seabed Authority. But if you care about the environment, you should know whether the deep ocean is mined fairly and safely.

And you cannot talk about deep sea mining without mentioning Ambassador Duncan Muhumuza Laki. Known as "Uncle D' in his circles, Laki comes from the hills of Ndeija in Rwampara, Uganda, a place about as far from the ocean as you can get. And for the last two decades, he has been one of the most influential voices in deciding who gets to dig up the floor of the deep sea, and under what rules.

What is the deep sea, and who owns it?

The deep ocean floor beyond any country's waters is called "the Area." Under international law, it does not belong to any nation. It belongs to everyone. It is officially known as the "common heritage of humankind." That sounds noble, but it creates a difficult problem. The deep seabed is rich with valuable minerals needed for electric car batteries, mobile phones, and renewable energy technology. Countries and companies want to mine them. But who decides the rules? Who makes sure mining does not destroy fragile ecosystems that scientists barely understand? That is where the International Seabed Authority comes in. Based in Kingston, Jamaica, it is the little-known organisation that acts as the steward of the deep ocean. And since 2025, Ambassador Laki has been its Council president, guiding it through the most intense negotiations in its history.

From Rwampara to the world

How does a lawyer from a landlocked country become a global authority on deep-sea mining? The answer is 40 years of public service, including 20 years working in international diplomacy. Ambassador Laki has served on the Authority's Finance Committee for two terms, eventually becoming its vice chairperson. He has been Uganda's Deputy Permanent Representative to the United Nations in New York. He has served on the Human Rights Committee. Along the way, he has built a reputation as someone who can take the most complex, technical arguments and turn them into fair outcomes.

The breakthrough of 2025

The year 2025 was a turning point. As president of the Council, Ambassador Laki presided over the 13th session of the Authority. The task was massive; finalise the "Mining Code," the set of rules that will govern how, or whether, deep-sea mining begins. Many people thought it could not be done. The interests involved are wildly different. Wealthy industrialised countries want access to minerals. Small island nations, terrified of ocean destruction, want a near-total ban. Mining companies want clear rules so they can invest. Environmentalists want the seabed left untouched.

Under Ambassador Laki's leadership, the Council achieved what many called impossible; they completed the second reading of the draft exploitation regulations. He used a tool called the "Friends of the President" mechanism to build agreement on difficult issues such as protecting underwater cables and preventing corruption. The final draft is not perfect, but it is balanced. It is a vision for sustainable mining, not a free-for-all.

A voice for the landlocked and the vulnerable

Throughout his career, Ambassador Laki has championed one core idea; the treasures of the deep sea must benefit all countries, not just those with coastlines. Uganda became a party to the UN Convention on the Law of the Sea in 1990. As a landlocked country, Uganda has no direct stake in ocean territory. But under the "common heritage" principle, Ugandans have as much right to benefit from deep-sea minerals as anyone else. Ambassador Laki often says: "When it comes to the resources of the deep sea, all countries are landlocked." No nation owns the deep ocean floor. It belongs to everyone, or it belongs to no one.

This philosophy puts him in constant dialogue with Small Island Developing States, who fear deep-sea mining will destroy their ocean ecosystems. Rather than dismissing their concerns, Ambassador Laki has worked to incorporate them. His legal background on the Human Rights Committee informs this balance. In one notable case, he wrote a dissenting opinion arguing that environmental threats pose direct and immediate dangers to human dignity. That same precautionary approach now guides his work on the seabed.

A new day for the deep seabed

Under Ambassador Laki's watch, the United Nations established the International Day of the Deep Seabed, observed on November 1. It is a small recognition of a vast, hidden world that most people will never see but which affects everyone.

As the world faces increasing pressure to mine the deep ocean for battery metals and rare earth elements, Ambassador Laki remains a stabilising force. He is not a hero who makes grand speeches. He is a diplomat who runs patient meetings, builds quiet consensus, and reminds everyone that the deep seabed is not a treasure chest to be looted. It is a legacy held in trust for future generations.

Beyond the Mining Code

Ambassador Laki's influence extends beyond the Authority's headquarters in Jamaica. At the United Nations in New York, he has been a key voice in the discussions about a new treaty on marine biodiversity beyond national borders. He has consistently argued that the new treaty should "not undermine" the 30 years of experience already built up by the Seabed Authority.

He also serves on the Partnership Fund Board and is helping to operationalise the Economic Planning Commission. These may sound like dry titles, but they are where the real work happens; deciding how mining revenues might be shared, how to protect the most vulnerable ecosystems, and how to ensure that deep-sea mining, if it happens, does not repeat the mistakes of land-based mining in poor countries.

The quiet giant from Rwampara

Ambassador Laki is now 32 years into a legal and public service career. He has led Uganda's delegations to countless international meetings. He has facilitated complex negotiations in the African Group and the United Nations Security Council, where he served as legal adviser during Uganda's term in 2009-2010. He has worked on children and armed conflict, and on the protection of civilians.

value.Title: New York launches a new onshore renewable energy procurement tender

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHC-0R73-RV7M-R2G9-00000-00New York launches a new onshore renewable energy procurement tender2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:49ZLexisNexisNew York launches a new onshore renewable energy procurement tenderNew Delhi


May 06 --

The New York State Energy Research and Development Authority (NYSERDA) has launched the 10th annual Renewable Energy Standard solicitation, RESRFP26-1, to accelerate clean energy deployment across New York. The solicitation, issued on April 24, 2026, seeks Tier 1-eligible renewable energy certificates (RECs) from projects with a near-term path to commercial operation. The procurement process will follow a two-step structure comprising a step one eligibility application and a step two competitive bid proposal process. This solicitation process aims to procure renewable energy certificates from eligible mature land-based projects, such as wind, hydroelectric and solar energy projects. 

Furthermore, under step two, NYSERDA will evaluate projects based on benchmark REC pricing, minimum threshold requirements, and additional price and non-price factors. Step one eligibility applications are due by May 19, 2026, while step two non-price and price submissions are due on June 25 and July 30, 2026, respectively. Developers must register through the solicitation portal to participate in the process. Initial project awards are expected by September 2026. 

New York aims for an emissions-free economy by 2050, including in the energy, buildings, transportation, and waste sectors.

Its current pipeline of large-scale renewable energy projects includes 61 solar, land-based wind, hydroelectric and offshore wind projects which are expected to add over 9 GW of clean power to the grid once operational.

value.Title: ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. - SECTOR SPOTLIGHT: KEY TRENDS AND PEER HIGHLIGHTS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH6-TKT3-S1FS-6527-00000-00ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. - SECTOR SPOTLIGHT: KEY TRENDS AND PEER HIGHLIGHTS2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:49ZLexisNexisSTOCKECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. - SECTOR SPOTLIGHT: KEY TRENDS AND PEER HIGHLIGHTS

DASHBOARD: ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD.

PermID4296560718Websitehttp://www.ecotricity.co.ukIndustryEnergy & Renewables

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. [3 Press Releases]

+ ECOTALK REINTRODUCE BEAVERS TO EAST SUSSEX AFTER A 400-YEAR ABSENCE

We've got amazing news from Ecotalk this spring with the release of a family of (extremely cute!) beavers into Bowyers Wood, East Sussex.

Chris Packham, Ecotalk's Chief Ecologist, was there to help the beavers get settled in. He said: "I just can't remember when I was last so excited about meeting a rodent! Big, fantastic rodents! What a fantastic day for Bowyers. And massive thanks to all our Ecotalk customers for making this possible."

Source: Company Website

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD.: GOOD NEWS AND BAD NEWS: OUR BREAKING THE LINK CAMPAIGN IS WORKING - BUT WE NEED TO GO FURTHER

Britain's energy market is broken. For years, we've been campaigning to "break the link" between the soaring cost of fossil gas and the price of the cheap, green electricity we generate right here in Britain.

Now, the message is finally hitting home in the corridors of Westminster.

There was a flurry of excitement in mid-April 2026 as the chancellor, Rachel Reeves, announced that the government was looking at ways to break the link between electricity and fossil gas prices.

Source: Company Website

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. OUR DAY WITH THE YOUNG GREEN BRITON CHALLENGE

Four members of the Ecotricity team recently took the hot seats as the judges at Stroud High School for entries to the Young Green Briton Challenge: a national programme that transforms students' climate ideas into real-world action.

For the 'Eco Dragon's Den', our team drew on their real-world expertise in green energy and sustainability. They used this to guide and challenge students on the impact and feasibility of their projects, offering constructive feedback to help sharpen each idea.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ OCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP [3 Press Releases]

+ OCTOPUS ENERGY GENERATION TO INVEST $500 MILLION TO REMOVE POLLUTING CO₂ FROM THE ATMOSPHERE

Major US deal to help cut up to 50 million tonnes of CO₂ - equivalent to eliminating the annual greenhouse gas emissions of New York City

Carbon credits to be snapped up by Google, Meta and McKinsey

Pushes Octopus closer to its ambitious $2bn US investment target, with a significant proportion in California's clean tech scene

London / San Francisco, 30 April 2026 - Octopus Energy Generation, one of Europe's leading renewables investors, is ramping up efforts to slash CO₂ pollution at scale - inking a major US deal that will help remove up to 50 million tonnes of carbon dioxide from the air.

Octopus's fund management team is set to invest $500 million in afforestation and reforestation projects in the US developed by public benefit and climate technology company Living Carbon.

Source: Company Website

+ POWERED UP: OCTOPUS AND MUIRHOUSE SLASH ENERGY BILLS FOR SCOTTISH SOCIAL HOMES BY £200

Muirhouse becomes one of Scotland's first housing associations to roll out Octopus Energy's groundbreaking Tenant Power tariff

UK's first green tech energy tariff that rewards both social housing landlords and tenants

Slashes electricity bills for social housing tenants by around £200 a year

Helping green home upgrades deliver real returns for landlords

London/Edinburgh, 29th April 2026 - Octopus Energy, the UK's largest energy supplier, has partnered with Muirhouse Housing Association to bring its groundbreaking Tenant Power tariff to over 30 social homes in Scotland.

Source: Company Website

+ 1 MILLION CUSTOMERS FOR EVERY TENTACLE: OCTOPUS ENERGY SUPERCHARGES TO 8 MILLION CUSTOMERS IN THE UK

The UK's largest energy supplier announces hitting 8 million home energy customers, with 2500 new customers joining daily

Market leading support for customers now includes You Pay We Pay, OctoAssist fund and the extension of their electric blanket scheme for Winter 2026

The milestone coincides with a doubling in heat pump sales and a 0.75 rise in solar sales as households shift to trusting in home grown power

London, 24th April 2026 - Octopus Energy has surpassed 8 million customers in the UK, further cementing its position as the nation's largest energy supplier.

With 2500 customers switching to Octopus every day, the gap has widened since Octopus claimed the top spot in January 2025, the first time the market lead had changed hands since privatisation in 1986.

Source: Company Website

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHD-9093-SDNH-R225-00000-00ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. - RECENT SECTOR HIGHLIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:49ZLexisNexisSTOCKECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. - RECENT SECTOR HIGHLIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD.

PermID4296560718Websitehttp://www.ecotricity.co.ukIndustryEnergy & Renewables

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. [3 Press Releases]

+ ECOTALK REINTRODUCE BEAVERS TO EAST SUSSEX AFTER A 400-YEAR ABSENCE

We've got amazing news from Ecotalk this spring with the release of a family of (extremely cute!) beavers into Bowyers Wood, East Sussex.

Chris Packham, Ecotalk's Chief Ecologist, was there to help the beavers get settled in. He said: "I just can't remember when I was last so excited about meeting a rodent! Big, fantastic rodents! What a fantastic day for Bowyers. And massive thanks to all our Ecotalk customers for making this possible."

Source: Company Website

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD.: GOOD NEWS AND BAD NEWS: OUR BREAKING THE LINK CAMPAIGN IS WORKING - BUT WE NEED TO GO FURTHER

Britain's energy market is broken. For years, we've been campaigning to "break the link" between the soaring cost of fossil gas and the price of the cheap, green electricity we generate right here in Britain.

Now, the message is finally hitting home in the corridors of Westminster.

There was a flurry of excitement in mid-April 2026 as the chancellor, Rachel Reeves, announced that the government was looking at ways to break the link between electricity and fossil gas prices.

Source: Company Website

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. OUR DAY WITH THE YOUNG GREEN BRITON CHALLENGE

Four members of the Ecotricity team recently took the hot seats as the judges at Stroud High School for entries to the Young Green Briton Challenge: a national programme that transforms students' climate ideas into real-world action.

For the 'Eco Dragon's Den', our team drew on their real-world expertise in green energy and sustainability. They used this to guide and challenge students on the impact and feasibility of their projects, offering constructive feedback to help sharpen each idea.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ OCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP [3 Press Releases]

+ OCTOPUS ENERGY GENERATION TO INVEST $500 MILLION TO REMOVE POLLUTING CO₂ FROM THE ATMOSPHERE

Major US deal to help cut up to 50 million tonnes of CO₂ - equivalent to eliminating the annual greenhouse gas emissions of New York City

Carbon credits to be snapped up by Google, Meta and McKinsey

Pushes Octopus closer to its ambitious $2bn US investment target, with a significant proportion in California's clean tech scene

London / San Francisco, 30 April 2026 - Octopus Energy Generation, one of Europe's leading renewables investors, is ramping up efforts to slash CO₂ pollution at scale - inking a major US deal that will help remove up to 50 million tonnes of carbon dioxide from the air.

Octopus's fund management team is set to invest $500 million in afforestation and reforestation projects in the US developed by public benefit and climate technology company Living Carbon.

Source: Company Website

+ POWERED UP: OCTOPUS AND MUIRHOUSE SLASH ENERGY BILLS FOR SCOTTISH SOCIAL HOMES BY £200

Muirhouse becomes one of Scotland's first housing associations to roll out Octopus Energy's groundbreaking Tenant Power tariff

UK's first green tech energy tariff that rewards both social housing landlords and tenants

Slashes electricity bills for social housing tenants by around £200 a year

Helping green home upgrades deliver real returns for landlords

London/Edinburgh, 29th April 2026 - Octopus Energy, the UK's largest energy supplier, has partnered with Muirhouse Housing Association to bring its groundbreaking Tenant Power tariff to over 30 social homes in Scotland.

Source: Company Website

+ 1 MILLION CUSTOMERS FOR EVERY TENTACLE: OCTOPUS ENERGY SUPERCHARGES TO 8 MILLION CUSTOMERS IN THE UK

The UK's largest energy supplier announces hitting 8 million home energy customers, with 2500 new customers joining daily

Market leading support for customers now includes You Pay We Pay, OctoAssist fund and the extension of their electric blanket scheme for Winter 2026

The milestone coincides with a doubling in heat pump sales and a 0.75 rise in solar sales as households shift to trusting in home grown power

London, 24th April 2026 - Octopus Energy has surpassed 8 million customers in the UK, further cementing its position as the nation's largest energy supplier.

With 2500 customers switching to Octopus every day, the gap has widened since Octopus claimed the top spot in January 2025, the first time the market lead had changed hands since privatisation in 1986.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JH6-9SW3-SH32-B44K-00000-00ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. - THE RECENT PEER NEWS2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:49ZLexisNexisSTOCKECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. - THE RECENT PEER NEWS

DASHBOARD: ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD.

PermID4296560718Websitehttp://www.ecotricity.co.ukIndustryEnergy & Renewables

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. [3 Press Releases]

+ ECOTALK REINTRODUCE BEAVERS TO EAST SUSSEX AFTER A 400-YEAR ABSENCE

We've got amazing news from Ecotalk this spring with the release of a family of (extremely cute!) beavers into Bowyers Wood, East Sussex.

Chris Packham, Ecotalk's Chief Ecologist, was there to help the beavers get settled in. He said: "I just can't remember when I was last so excited about meeting a rodent! Big, fantastic rodents! What a fantastic day for Bowyers. And massive thanks to all our Ecotalk customers for making this possible."

Source: Company Website

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD.: GOOD NEWS AND BAD NEWS: OUR BREAKING THE LINK CAMPAIGN IS WORKING - BUT WE NEED TO GO FURTHER

Britain's energy market is broken. For years, we've been campaigning to "break the link" between the soaring cost of fossil gas and the price of the cheap, green electricity we generate right here in Britain.

Now, the message is finally hitting home in the corridors of Westminster.

There was a flurry of excitement in mid-April 2026 as the chancellor, Rachel Reeves, announced that the government was looking at ways to break the link between electricity and fossil gas prices.

Source: Company Website

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. OUR DAY WITH THE YOUNG GREEN BRITON CHALLENGE

Four members of the Ecotricity team recently took the hot seats as the judges at Stroud High School for entries to the Young Green Briton Challenge: a national programme that transforms students' climate ideas into real-world action.

For the 'Eco Dragon's Den', our team drew on their real-world expertise in green energy and sustainability. They used this to guide and challenge students on the impact and feasibility of their projects, offering constructive feedback to help sharpen each idea.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ OCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP [3 Press Releases]

+ OCTOPUS ENERGY GENERATION TO INVEST $500 MILLION TO REMOVE POLLUTING CO₂ FROM THE ATMOSPHERE

Major US deal to help cut up to 50 million tonnes of CO₂ - equivalent to eliminating the annual greenhouse gas emissions of New York City

Carbon credits to be snapped up by Google, Meta and McKinsey

Pushes Octopus closer to its ambitious $2bn US investment target, with a significant proportion in California's clean tech scene

London / San Francisco, 30 April 2026 - Octopus Energy Generation, one of Europe's leading renewables investors, is ramping up efforts to slash CO₂ pollution at scale - inking a major US deal that will help remove up to 50 million tonnes of carbon dioxide from the air.

Octopus's fund management team is set to invest $500 million in afforestation and reforestation projects in the US developed by public benefit and climate technology company Living Carbon.

Source: Company Website

+ POWERED UP: OCTOPUS AND MUIRHOUSE SLASH ENERGY BILLS FOR SCOTTISH SOCIAL HOMES BY £200

Muirhouse becomes one of Scotland's first housing associations to roll out Octopus Energy's groundbreaking Tenant Power tariff

UK's first green tech energy tariff that rewards both social housing landlords and tenants

Slashes electricity bills for social housing tenants by around £200 a year

Helping green home upgrades deliver real returns for landlords

London/Edinburgh, 29th April 2026 - Octopus Energy, the UK's largest energy supplier, has partnered with Muirhouse Housing Association to bring its groundbreaking Tenant Power tariff to over 30 social homes in Scotland.

Source: Company Website

+ 1 MILLION CUSTOMERS FOR EVERY TENTACLE: OCTOPUS ENERGY SUPERCHARGES TO 8 MILLION CUSTOMERS IN THE UK

The UK's largest energy supplier announces hitting 8 million home energy customers, with 2500 new customers joining daily

Market leading support for customers now includes You Pay We Pay, OctoAssist fund and the extension of their electric blanket scheme for Winter 2026

The milestone coincides with a doubling in heat pump sales and a 0.75 rise in solar sales as households shift to trusting in home grown power

London, 24th April 2026 - Octopus Energy has surpassed 8 million customers in the UK, further cementing its position as the nation's largest energy supplier.

With 2500 customers switching to Octopus every day, the gap has widened since Octopus claimed the top spot in January 2025, the first time the market lead had changed hands since privatisation in 1986.

Source: Company Website

urn:contentItem:6JH5-5NH3-RWR1-N03W-00000-00National Grid Expands DLR Technology Across 585km of UK Transmission Routes2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:53:26ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSNational Grid Expands DLR Technology Across 585km of UK Transmission Routes

BRITISH DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED KINGDOM EDITION

05 May 2026 09:31 BST

National Grid PLC is set to expand its Dynamic Line Rating (DLR) technology to 585 kilometers of existing transmission routes across the UK. This initiative, announced on the 1st of May 2026, aims to deliver substantial savings for consumers, projected at up to £50 million in constraint costs over the next five years.

The DLR system utilizes advanced sensors to continuously monitor electricity transmission lines, calculating a dynamic line rating based on various factors, including wire specifications and weather conditions. This approach provides a more accurate assessment of the maximum power carrying capacity of transmission lines compared to traditional assumptions, which often underestimate their capabilities.

National Grid estimates that the implementation of DLR technology enables transmission lines to handle an average of 8% more electricity safely. The technology was initially tested in New York and Massachusetts in partnership with US-based provider LineVision before being introduced to the UK through a trial program in Cumbria that began in 2022. Following the success of the pilot, National Grid expanded the DLR system last year, adding an additional 275 kilometers of transmission routes.

The upcoming rollout will be conducted in partnership with LineVision, Belgian grid monitoring firm Ampacimon, and Danish cybersecurity company Heimdall. National Grid anticipates that the majority of the upgrades will be completed by 2028, with plans to incorporate drone technology for sensor installation on live power lines. Alice Delahunty, president of National Grid Electricity Transmission, emphasized that these innovations will not only enhance grid capacity but also facilitate the integration of renewable energy into the system.

The expansion of the grid's operational infrastructure is especially critical given the increasing challenges of grid capacity in the UK. Earlier this year, Foresight Solar Fund's investment manager, Toby Virno, highlighted the alarming rise in curtailment costs, which have reached £2.3 billion as the UK continues to commission more renewable energy capacity than its current grid can effectively accommodate. This DLR expansion by National Grid could be a pivotal move in addressing this pressing issue.

SECTION 1 NATIONAL GRID RECENT PRESS RELEASES

SECTION 2 NATIONAL GRID ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 NATIONAL GRID RECENT PRESS RELEASES

November 16, 2025: National Grid announces dividend

National Grid today announced an interim dividend of GBX16.35 per share. The ex-dividend date is Thursday, November 20 and it is payable on Tuesday, January 13, 2026.

July 09, 2025: National Grid announces AGM

National Grid has announced its Annual General Meeting will take place on Wednesday, July 09.

May 18, 2025: National Grid announces dividend

National Grid today announced a final dividend of GBX30.88 per share. The ex-dividend date is Thursday, May 29, 2025 and it is payable on Thursday, July 17.

SECTION 2 NATIONAL GRID ACTIVITIES

Activities

National Grid PLC is a leading multinational utility company headquartered in the United Kingdom, with operations spanning the UK and northeastern United States. Its core business activities focus on the transmission and distribution of electricity and gas, ensuring reliable energy supply to over 20 million customers while maintaining critical infrastructure for national energy security. The company is actively investing in infrastructure to support the transition to a low-carbon economy, including grid modernization and renewable energy integration. Among its fastest-growing segments are offshore wind projects, which are expanding rapidly to meet rising demand for sustainable energy, as well as smart grid technologies and electric vehicle charging networks. These initiatives position National Grid as a key player in advancing energy efficiency and decarbonization, driving long-term growth through strategic investments and innovation. It is the largest Gas, water & multiutilities company by market capitalisation in the United Kingdom.

value.Title: MN8 Energy Closes Tolling Agreement, Expands Public EV Charging Footprint in San Jose , California

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHT-FN43-S35T-Y4F6-00000-00MN8 Energy Closes Tolling Agreement, Expands Public EV Charging Footprint in San Jose, California2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:49ZLexisNexisMN8 Energy Closes Tolling Agreement, Expands Public EV Charging Footprint in San Jose, California

Key Highlights:

* MN8 Energy closes tolling agreement with San Jose Clean Energy for 40 DCFC ports.

* 40 affordable DCFC ports to be installed across five city-owned sites in San Jose.

* MN8's DES platform provides proven track record of EV charging infrastructure deployment.

Original Press Release:

SAN JOSE, California, May 5 -- MN8 Energy LLC issued the following news release:

- The agreement brings 40 affordable direct current fast charging (DCFC) ports to underserved communities.

MN8 Energy LLC ("MN8") today announced a new tolling agreement with San Jose Clean Energy ("SJCE") to design, build, own, and operate 40 direct-current fast-charging (DCFC) ports across five city-owned sites.

Under the agreement, MN8 is responsible for funding project costs and supporting operational performance, while SJCE retains control over branding and retail pricing. Revenue is collected and reconciled against a fixed fee structure, creating a predictable framework for both parties.

The novel tolling agreement is modeled after the way California utilities and CCAs currently procure power assets, removing the complexity of acquiring EV charging infrastructure. The model eliminates ownership risks for public agencies, lowering the barrier to deployment.

"This is EV charging procurement done the way the industry already knows how to work," said Alan Dowdell, Head of Distributed Energy Solutions (DES) at MN8 Energy. "We've built this model to work for any CCA looking to procure EV charging. San Jose is our first stop, we're excited to continue building across the state."

MN8's DESplatformbrings a proventrack recordofprovidingEV charging infrastructure in partnership with cities and in service of underserved communities.In July 2025, the companyannounceditsGateway to Hunts Point project, a first-of-its-kind freight EV charging hub in New York's South Bronx. The projectreflects MN8's commitment to deploying infrastructure investment in underserved communities.

The deployment in San Jose covers five city-owned sites, totaling 8 MW of capacity and featuring 20 dual-port chargers across neighborhoods with limited access to public EV charging infrastructure. Construction is expected to begin in late 2026, with stations coming online in early 2027.

About MN8

MN8 Energy serves enterprise customers on their journey to an electrified, decarbonized world by providing renewable energy and related services. With approximately 4 gigawatts of operational and under-construction solar projects, 1.1 gigawatt hours of battery energy storage capacity comprised of over 874 projects across 29 states, and more than 100 high-power EV charging stations across 26 states, MN8 is one of the largest and most sophisticated independent solar energy and energy storage power producers in the United States. MN8 is headquartered in New York and has offices in South Florida, Dallas, and Madrid. Learn more at MN8.com.

[Category: Energy, Real Estate, Partnerships, Electric Vehicles, ESG and Sustainability]

Source: MN8 Energy LLC

value.Title: Aspen Power Acquires Operating New Jersey Rooftop Solar Portfolio Projects in PSE&G territory acquired from industrial real estate developer

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH0-55G3-RXMV-P0BC-00000-00Aspen Power Acquires Operating New Jersey Rooftop Solar Portfolio2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:59:28ZLexisNexisAspen Power Acquires Operating New Jersey Rooftop Solar PortfolioProjects in PSE&G territory acquired from industrial real estate developerNEW YORK

Aspen Power , a leading distributed generation platform building the clean energy future, today announced the acquisition of two operating rooftop solar projects totaling 6.44 megawatts direct current (MWdc) from multigenerational real estate developer The Avidan Group. The projects, located in PSE&G territory in New Jersey, have operated for more than a decade and produce over 7 million kilowatt-hours annually, equivalent to the annual electricity use of more than 1,000 homes or charging nearly 400 million smartphones each year.

This press release features multimedia. View the full release here: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260504631431/en/

Aspen Power acquired an operating New Jersey industrial rooftop solar portfolio. Photo courtesy of The Avidan Group.

The solar projects were built using American-made materials and provide a majority of the buildings' electricity needs. Originally developed through PSE&G's Solar Loan Program, the systems were designed to deliver long-term energy cost stability.

"At Aspen Power, we help real estate owners transition out of solar ownership while ensuring their systems continue to perform," said Jeff Martin, Vice President, Acquisitions and Structuring, Aspen Power. "We specialize in stepping in as long-term owners so our partners can stay focused on their core business while unlocking additional value."

Aspen Power is actively acquiring legacy solar assets like these as part of its disciplined growth strategy. By bringing experienced ownership and asset management to operating projects, the company helps ensure continued performance while providing real estate owners with a clear path to monetize legacy solar assets and reduce operational burden.

"We're proud of the role these systems have played in helping reduce energy costs and improve building performance over the past decade plus," said Josh Avidan, Managing Member at The Avidan Group. "Aspen Power brings the scale, operational depth, and long-term investment horizon needed to steward these projects into their next phase while we continue to focus on growing and managing our real estate portfolio."

The acquisition reflects Aspen Power's continued focus on acquiring assets in core distributed generation markets and partnering with real estate owners seeking reliable solar ownership solutions.

About Aspen Power

Aspen Power is a distributed energy generation platform with the dual mission of accelerating and democratizing decarbonization. We partner with businesses, communities, and others in the industry to develop, construct, own and operate renewable energy assets. Our experienced team is passionate about solving our clients' energy challenges to deliver a sustainable future throughout the U.S. For more information, please visit aspenpower.com .

About The Avidan Group

The Avidan Group is a family-owned and operated industrial real estate developer headquartered in Elizabeth, New Jersey, with more than 60 years of multi-generational experience, focused on the development, ownership, and hands-on management of high-quality, strategically located single- and multi-purpose industrial properties. Today, The Avidan Group actively manages approximately one million square feet of industrial real estate assets, including warehouse and distribution facilities, flex space, and outdoor storage, with a strong footprint in New Jersey, Maryland, Tennessee, and Florida. For more information, please visit www.theavidangroup.com .

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260504631431/en/

urn:contentItem:6JGJ-D6Y3-S1D8-3043-00000-00ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. - HIGHLIGHTS FROM OCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP AND PEER COMPANIES2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:49ZLexisNexisSTOCKECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. - HIGHLIGHTS FROM OCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP AND PEER COMPANIES

DASHBOARD: ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD.

PermID4296560718Websitehttp://www.ecotricity.co.ukIndustryEnergy & Renewables

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. [3 Press Releases]

+ ECOTALK REINTRODUCE BEAVERS TO EAST SUSSEX AFTER A 400-YEAR ABSENCE

We've got amazing news from Ecotalk this spring with the release of a family of (extremely cute!) beavers into Bowyers Wood, East Sussex.

Chris Packham, Ecotalk's Chief Ecologist, was there to help the beavers get settled in. He said: "I just can't remember when I was last so excited about meeting a rodent! Big, fantastic rodents! What a fantastic day for Bowyers. And massive thanks to all our Ecotalk customers for making this possible."

Source: Company Website

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD.: GOOD NEWS AND BAD NEWS: OUR BREAKING THE LINK CAMPAIGN IS WORKING - BUT WE NEED TO GO FURTHER

Britain's energy market is broken. For years, we've been campaigning to "break the link" between the soaring cost of fossil gas and the price of the cheap, green electricity we generate right here in Britain.

Now, the message is finally hitting home in the corridors of Westminster.

There was a flurry of excitement in mid-April 2026 as the chancellor, Rachel Reeves, announced that the government was looking at ways to break the link between electricity and fossil gas prices.

Source: Company Website

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. OUR DAY WITH THE YOUNG GREEN BRITON CHALLENGE

Four members of the Ecotricity team recently took the hot seats as the judges at Stroud High School for entries to the Young Green Briton Challenge: a national programme that transforms students' climate ideas into real-world action.

For the 'Eco Dragon's Den', our team drew on their real-world expertise in green energy and sustainability. They used this to guide and challenge students on the impact and feasibility of their projects, offering constructive feedback to help sharpen each idea.

Source: Company Website

+ OCTOPUS ENERGY GENERATION TO INVEST $500 MILLION TO REMOVE POLLUTING CO₂ FROM THE ATMOSPHERE

Major US deal to help cut up to 50 million tonnes of CO₂ - equivalent to eliminating the annual greenhouse gas emissions of New York City

Carbon credits to be snapped up by Google, Meta and McKinsey

Pushes Octopus closer to its ambitious $2bn US investment target, with a significant proportion in California's clean tech scene

London / San Francisco, 30 April 2026 - Octopus Energy Generation, one of Europe's leading renewables investors, is ramping up efforts to slash CO₂ pollution at scale - inking a major US deal that will help remove up to 50 million tonnes of carbon dioxide from the air.

Octopus's fund management team is set to invest $500 million in afforestation and reforestation projects in the US developed by public benefit and climate technology company Living Carbon.

Source: Company Website

+ POWERED UP: OCTOPUS AND MUIRHOUSE SLASH ENERGY BILLS FOR SCOTTISH SOCIAL HOMES BY £200

Muirhouse becomes one of Scotland's first housing associations to roll out Octopus Energy's groundbreaking Tenant Power tariff

UK's first green tech energy tariff that rewards both social housing landlords and tenants

Slashes electricity bills for social housing tenants by around £200 a year

Helping green home upgrades deliver real returns for landlords

London/Edinburgh, 29th April 2026 - Octopus Energy, the UK's largest energy supplier, has partnered with Muirhouse Housing Association to bring its groundbreaking Tenant Power tariff to over 30 social homes in Scotland.

Source: Company Website

+ 1 MILLION CUSTOMERS FOR EVERY TENTACLE: OCTOPUS ENERGY SUPERCHARGES TO 8 MILLION CUSTOMERS IN THE UK

The UK's largest energy supplier announces hitting 8 million home energy customers, with 2500 new customers joining daily

Market leading support for customers now includes You Pay We Pay, OctoAssist fund and the extension of their electric blanket scheme for Winter 2026

The milestone coincides with a doubling in heat pump sales and a 0.75 rise in solar sales as households shift to trusting in home grown power

London, 24th April 2026 - Octopus Energy has surpassed 8 million customers in the UK, further cementing its position as the nation's largest energy supplier.

With 2500 customers switching to Octopus every day, the gap has widened since Octopus claimed the top spot in January 2025, the first time the market lead had changed hands since privatisation in 1986.

Source: Company Website

value.Title: Hearing for proposed solar project in Massena and Brasher draws no public comments Still time to weigh-in, officials say
value.Overview: North Country Now (Potsdam, New York), NEWS, 428words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH4-BS13-S0B3-H34X-00000-00Hearing for proposed solar project in Massena and Brasher draws no public comments2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:49ZLexisNexisNEWSHearing for proposed solar project in Massena and Brasher draws no public commentsStill time to weigh-in, officials sayJEFF CHUDZINSKI

MASSENA -- A proposed 200-megawatt solar farm in the towns of Brasher and Massena is one step closer to reality after a 15-minute public hearing with the Public Service Commission drew no comments.

The proposed Two Rivers Solar Farm has already received a siting permitting through the state's Office of Renewable Energy Siting and Electric Transmission, however a Certificate of Public Convenience and Necessity (CPCN) is still required.

The hearing held on April 29 was aimed at allowing the public to weigh-in prior to PSC board members deciding on the issuance of a CPCN, which is a regulatory permit private companies are required to obtain before building, operating or expanding public services, officials said.

If the regulatory permit is issued, Boralex could build the solar farm, which will encompass roughly 1,000 acres while creating around 150 full-time jobs during the construction phase.

Once online, the company would employ two or three full-time staffers to maintain the array.

As part of the CPCN permitting process, members of the public can voice support or opposition for such projects, with one of the primary focuses being environmental and economic impacts.

"I would just like to thank anybody who has turned out to participate in this process. You're taking time out of your schedule to make your voice heard, which I appreciate. The public statement hearings inject information that as a commissioner I find very helpful in better understanding items that might come before us," Commissioner John Maggiore said April 29.

Though Administrative Law Judge Leah Amyot issued the siting permit on Feb. 27, laws state there is a requirement for the public comment period on the petition to ensure the project is economically feasible, can be properly maintained, will be safe in building and operation and will provide adequate and reliable service.

Ultimately, the project would have to serve the public interest, according to the law.

Amyot said comments are still allowed but must be received by May 6.

To make a comment, visit https://dps.ny.gov/.

Under "Commission Files," click "File Search," then enter "25-E-0616" in the search number box.

Click on "Post Comments" at the top of the page.

For those who wish to use an alternative method, comments can be mailed to Michelle Phillips, Secretary, Public Service Commission, Three Empire State Plaza, Albany, New York, 12223-1350.

To submit a comment via phone call, call the commission's Opinion Line at 1-800-335-2120. The line is set to receive in-state calls 24-hours per day, officials said.

Jeff CHUDZINSKI headshot

value.Title: EV Charging Jobs Program Coming to Brooklyn Navy Yard Brooklyn Navy Yard launches a new EV training program to prepare New Yorkers for clean energy jobs in a growing industry.
value.Overview: BK Reader (Brooklyn, New York), BUSINESS & INNOVATION, 415words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGX-2TF3-SF61-T4HC-00000-00EV Charging Jobs Program Coming to Brooklyn Navy Yard2026-05-03T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:49ZLexisNexisBUSINESS & INNOVATIONEV Charging Jobs Program Coming to Brooklyn Navy YardBrooklyn Navy Yard launches a new EV training program to prepare New Yorkers for clean energy jobs in a growing industry.

A new workforce training program at the Brooklyn Navy Yard is set to prepare New Yorkers for jobs maintaining and repairing electric vehicle charging systems as demand for clean energy infrastructure continues to grow.

The Brooklyn Navy Yard Development Corp. has been awarded $450,000 from the New York Power Authority to develop the initiative, part of a broader $5 million statewide effort to align job training with New York's transition to renewable energy.

The program, created in partnership with SmarterHelp, is expected to launch later this year following the buildout of a dedicated Smart Lab at the Navy Yard. The facility will include electric vehicle charging units and hands-on training equipment designed to mirror real-world conditions.

"Workforce development should be driven by actual jobs and designed around the lives people actually lead," said Deputy Mayor for Economic Justice Julie Su. "By making this program part-time, accessible and rooted in a growing industry, we're ensuring that NYC is expanding clean energy capacity while simultaneously creating lasting economic mobility for New Yorkers who have too often been shut out of the promise of a good job."

Participants will receive technical instruction in electrical systems, energy fundamentals and charging equipment maintenance, along with preparation for industry certifications. The goal is to connect graduates directly to employment opportunities in a rapidly expanding sector.

Organizers have structured the program as part-time, making it accessible to working adults and those unable to commit to full-time training. The approach is intended to lower barriers to entry while expanding access to stable, well-paying jobs tied to the clean energy economy.

The initiative comes as New York continues to scale up its electric vehicle charging network, increasing the need for skilled technicians to support installation, maintenance and long-term reliability.

"The SmarterHelp Training Center is aligned with the Navy Yard's vision of creating pathways into skilled jobs for underserved New Yorkers," said Lindsay Greene, president and chief executive officer of the Brooklyn Navy Yard Development Corp. "By meeting people where they are, we are ensuring that the future of the green workforce is both sustainable and inclusive."

New York's clean energy sector now includes about 185,000 jobs, with continued growth expected as the state invests in climate-focused infrastructure and workforce development.

value.Title: Hospital powers up with green energy

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK2-PJJ3-S8H2-1495-00000-00Hospital powers up with green energy2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:49ZLexisNexisNEWSPg. 25Hospital powers up with green energy

Solar panels have been installed at the site of Ninewells Hospital as a step towards achieving net-zero by 2040.

Located at the MacKenzie building, the new photovoltaic solar panels are designed to generate clean, renewable electricity on-site.

Installation has been undertaken in the hope of reducing NHS Tayside's carbon footprint by 15.2 tonnes of CO2 every year, which equates to 10 return flights from London to New York, or the equivalent of 25 households' typical annual electricity consumption.

This project joins recent installations of solar panels at Westgate Health Centre and Abbey Health Centre in Arbroath.

Described as a "key milestone", the organisation has said this step will contribute to Scotland's efforts to tackle climate change.

The project was in partnership with Vital Energi through the CEF Energy contract on the Ninewells campus and funded by the Central Energy Efficiency Fund - a Scottish Government initiative established in 2005 to support energysaving measures in public bodies.

NHS Tayside's associate director of facilities Mark Anderson said: "Every milestone like this reflects the commitment and collaboration that is driving a more sustainable future for healthcare in Tayside."

John Ruddy, Richard Mills, Jonathan Milne and David Gibson at the new solar panels.

value.Title: Skyview Ventures integrates monitoring program for beekeeping on solar project sites

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHC-0C53-RS65-K1KM-00000-00Skyview Ventures integrates monitoring program for beekeeping on solar project sites2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:49ZLexisNexisSkyview Ventures integrates monitoring program for beekeeping on solar project sites

Renewable energy investor Skyview Ventures is deploying an environmental monitoring program across its portfolio of solar projects to manage pollinator activity on these sites. Through its solar subsidiary, Davis Hill Development, Skyview is integrating HiveTracks biodiversity monitoring on eight solar projects in New York, West Virginia, Tennessee and Connecticut. Skyview invested in HiveTracks and tested the platform on some project sites, and plans to expand to the rest in 2026.

This partnership reflects our broader vision that renewable energy projects should be developed with long-term environmental stewardship in mind, said Andy Karetsky, CEO of Skyview Ventures. By integrating biodiversity monitoring, pollinator habitats and data-driven land management into our solar portfolio, we are demonstrating that solar projects can deliver environmental benefits beyond clean energy generation and play a meaningful role in supporting local ecosystems.

HiveTracks hires local beekeepers to work on these solar sites and supports them with proprietary monitoring. HiveTracks was developed to monitor ecosystem health and impacts of solar projects on the site over time, from pre-construction through operations all while producing honey. Honey produced on Skyview project sites in 2025 was donated to local food banks and given away as gifts.

With the data gathered on project sites regarding the health of pollinators, and plant diversity and abundance HiveTracks offers recommendations for proprietors to further improve local biodiversity.

Solar energy already provides environmental benefits, but with HiveTracks, we saw an opportunity to go further, said Maria Morales Ferrebus, solar development associate at Skyview Ventures. In our first year, the data provided valuable insights into ecosystem health across our sites. For example, we identified areas with low plant diversity, which can reduce resilience to drought conditions and increase erosion risk. By implementing HiveTracks recommendations, we can adapt our operations and maintenance practices to enhance plant diversity, improve soil health, and strengthen pollinator habitats.

Skyview stated in a press release that integrating HiveTrack has created a scalable modle supporting pollinator health and community agriculture, while generating guided data informing long-term land stewardship.

Solar sites represent a unique opportunity to support pollinators and monitor biodiversity at scale, said Max Rnzel, CEO of HiveTracks. Our partnership with Skyview Ventures demonstrates how beekeeping, environmental data, and renewable energy infrastructure can work together to create measurable environmental impact.

urn:contentItem:6JK2-K5D3-S2HV-T4VT-00000-00Primoris Services Corporation (NYSE:PRIM) Securities Probe Launched Over Q1 Performance and Revised EBITDA Guidance2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:49ZLexisNexisPrimoris Services Corporation (NYSE:PRIM) Securities Probe Launched Over Q1 Performance and Revised EBITDA Guidance

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

14 May 2026 02:43 EDT

NEW YORK CITY, NY / ACCESS Newswire / May 12, 2026 — Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC initiated a securities investigation on behalf of Primoris Services Corporation (NYSE:PRIM) shareholders. The company reported May 5, 2026 that first-quarter results were below estimates and that full-year adjusted EBITDA guidance was lowered from $560-$580 million to $480-$500 million, with the downgrade driven by reduced renewable-energy activity, delayed project starts, and higher renewable project costs. Following the earnings release, Primoris closed at $101.23 on May 6, 2026, down 50.11% from the prior close after a $101.69 per-share drop. Investors with knowledge are encouraged to visit bgandg.com/PRIM or call Peretz Bronstein or Nathan Miller at 917-590-0911. Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman emphasizes it represents investors on a contingency fee basis and has recovered hundreds of millions for investors nationwide; the firm notes that its work aims to restore capital and uphold market integrity. "Our practice centers on restoring investor capital and ensuring corporate accountability, which serves to uphold the essential integrity of the marketplace."

IN TODAY'S REPORT:

SECTION 1 PRIMORIS SERVICES RECENT STORIES ON DIVIDENDS:

SECTION 1 Primoris Services Recent Stories on Dividends:

May 08: Primoris Services quarterly dividends steady

Primoris Services today announced a quarterly dividend of 8.0c per share for Q1/2026; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q4/2025) dividend of 8.0c. The ex-dividend date is Tuesday, June 30, 2026 and the record date is Tuesday, June 30, 2026 and it is payable on Wednesday, July 15.

May 08: Primoris Services quarterly dividends steady

Primoris Services today announced a quarterly dividend of 8.0c per share for Q1/2026; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q4/2025) dividend of 8.0c. The ex-dividend date is Tuesday, June 30, 2026 and the record date is Tuesday, June 30, 2026 and it is payable on Wednesday, July 15.

May 08: Primoris Services quarterly dividends steady

Primoris Services today announced a quarterly dividend of 8.0c per share for Q1/2026; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q4/2025) dividend of 8.0c. The ex-dividend date is Tuesday, June 30, 2026 and the record date is Tuesday, June 30, 2026 and it is payable on Wednesday, July 15.

urn:contentItem:6JJG-P063-RW32-8136-00000-00Researchers' Work from Exponent Focuses on Renewable Energy (Designing Renewables for an Uncertain World: Adaptive Co-design Formulation and Marine Energy Case Study)2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:14ZLexisNexisENERGY - RENEWABLE ENERGYResearchers' Work from Exponent Focuses on Renewable Energy (Designing Renewables for an Uncertain World: Adaptive Co-design Formulation and Marine Energy Case Study)


2026 MAY 11 (NewsRx) -- By a News Reporter-Staff News Editor at Energy Daily News -- New research on Energy - Renewable Energy is the subject of a report. According to news reporting out of Chicago, Illinois, by NewsRx editors, research stated, "Renewable energy systems are subject to uncertainty arising both from modeling simplifications/imperfections and a stochastic environment. To maximize energy production under this uncertainty, real-time plant and controller adaptability can be introduced."


Financial support for this research came from NSF - Directorate for Engineering (ENG).
Our news journalists obtained a quote from the research from Exponent, "While the inclusion of plant and controller adaptability has been demonstrated to enhance the energetic performance of both wind and marine energy systems, this comes at an economic cost, due to the need for additional actuators and adaptive control software development. In this work, a co-design framework is presented to identify the system design, model refinement technique, and degree of plant and controller adaptability that minimize the expected levelized cost of energy (LCOE) of a renewable energy system. This framework is then applied to a detailed case study involving an installation of marine hydrokinetic (MHK) kites, using a detailed cost model and a computationally efficient surrogate model to estimate energetic performance. The study resulted in identified LCOE-optimal levels of plant and controller adaptability, in addition to an optimal level of model refinement following the plant freeze date (recognizing that this model refinement comes at a development cost). To fully realize the range of adaptation built into the design of the optimized kite system, a real-time extremum-seeking-based adaptation strategy is presented."
According to the news editors, the research concluded: "Through dynamic simulation results, we show that the energetic performance of the kite converges to the predictions generated by the surrogate model, thus validating the use of the surrogate model in the co-design optimization."
This research has been peer-reviewed.
For more information on this research see: Designing Renewables for an Uncertain World: Adaptive Co-design Formulation and Marine Energy Case Study. Journal of Dynamic Systems, Measurement, and Control, 2026;148(3). Journal of Dynamic Systems, Measurement, and Control can be contacted at: Asme, Two Park Ave, New York, NY 10016-5990, USA.
Our news journalists report that additional information may be obtained by contacting Jacob B. Fine, Exponent, Chicago, IL 60661, United States.
The direct object identifier (DOI) for that additional information is: https://doi.org/10.1115/1.4070410. This DOI is a link to an online electronic document that is either free or for purchase, and can be your direct source for a journal article and its citation.
Keywords for this news article include: Chicago, Illinois, United States, North and Central America, Energy, Oil & Gas, Renewable Energy, Exponent.
Our reports deliver fact-based news of research and discoveries from around the world. Copyright 2026, NewsRx LLC
